article_meta.title: Why the US needs China's rare earths16 October 2025ShareSaveAyeshea PereraAsia Digital Editor, Singapore ShareSaveGetty

reasoning: No attribution violations and several genuine author-prose biases identified, but some clear instances were missed and a couple of bias-type labels are suboptimal.

custom_reasoning.attribution_rule_compliance: All flagged passages are from the author’s prose; none are quotes or attributed statements.
custom_reasoning.false_positive_rate: Flagged items reflect loaded phrasing (e.g., ‘chokehold,’ ‘major blow,’ ‘hit the US hard,’ ‘had his eye on’) in the author’s voice; these are defensible as bias.
custom_reasoning.false_negative_rate: Missed several notable author-level biases, including ‘tightened its grip,’ ‘it’s clear this is something already on Trump’s mind,’ and ‘stand to be severely impacted.’
custom_reasoning.bias_type_accuracy: Some labels fit well (e.g., Sensationalism for ‘hit the US hard,’ Spin for ‘had his eye on’), but others are less precise: ‘chokehold’ fits Word Choice/Spin better than Opinion-as-Fact; ‘near monopoly’ is better cast as Word Choice/Spin than Subjective Qualifying Adjectives.
